reasoning: The market probability is 0.02, which reflects the extreme historical rarity of an album spending 17 consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200. In the modern era, no album has achieved 17 straight weeks (the closest recent phenomenons like Taylor Swift's 'The Tortured Poets Department' and Morgan Wallen's 'One Thing at a Time' both managed 15 consecutive weeks, while the 'Titanic' soundtrack achieved 16 consecutive). Reaching 17 straight weeks requires fending off all new major album releases for roughly four months, which is incredibly difficult in the streaming era. Given the lack of search tools to verify if this album has even been released or its current streak, relying on the base rate and the market's 2% estimate is the most sound approach. I am highly confident the probability is 0.02.
